title: Polybotrya serratifolia
taxonomy: Polybotrya serratifolia is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Polybotrya, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with many species found worldwide. This classification suggests that Polybotrya serratifolia is a member of a group of ferns that are likely to be epiphytic or terrestrial in habit. Further research into the specific characteristics and habitats of Polybotrya serratifolia may provide more information about its classification within the family Dryopteridaceae.
uses: Polybotrya serratifolia is a species of fern known for its medicinal properties in traditional medicine. In various cultures, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. Its leaves are often used in folk remedies to reduce swelling and promote healing.
